methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
ideaForge is a public-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Ankit Mehta, having raised $50M+ to date. The relevant comp universe consists of six private peers in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and Aurora Innovation. No pure-play public comparables exist in this narrow category.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The sector contains only four same-stage peers, and filtering by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints—below the minimum threshold for statistical reliability. Attempting an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and misrepresent the company's fundamentals relative to true peers operating in similar technical domains.

This limitation leaves ideaForge without a triangulated valuation range supported by the available data. Meaningful analysis would require either: expansion of the comp set to include adjacent autonomous systems verticals, acquisition of additional private market transactions in drones, or reliance on alternative methods (DCF, precedent M&A) not covered in the current dataset. The key uncertainty remains the thinness of comparable transactions at this stage and sector intersection, making any point estimate unreliable without additional methodological inputs or market data.
